Receiver-struct injection is independent of file order: a provider may
consume a handler struct whose route lives in a later-sorting file.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

func main() {
	if err := run(); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
-- web/a.go --
package web

type Foo struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewFoo(h *Handlers) *Foo { return &Foo{} }
-- web/b.go --
package web

import "net/http"

type API struct {
	Foo *Foo
}

//fabrik:http GET /api
func (a *API) Get(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- web/z.go --
package web

import "net/http"

type Handlers struct{}

//fabrik:http GET /h
func (h *Handlers) Get(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"net/http"
	"os"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router"

	"app/web"
)

func run() error {
	// Providers
	webHandlers := &web.Handlers{}
	webFoo := web.NewFoo(webHandlers)
	webAPI := &web.API{
		Foo: webFoo,
	}

	r := router.New()

	// Register
	r.Method("GET", "/api", webAPI.Get)
	r.Method("GET", "/h", webHandlers.Get)

	// Serve
	addr := ":8080"
	if p := os.Getenv("PORT"); p != "" {
		addr = ":" + p
	}
	return http.ListenAndServe(addr, r)
}
